briefing.common:
It's November 2026. The AI race is real, and the people inside it know it.

OpenBrain has just finished training Agent-1 — a powerful coding and research agent. Internally, it's giving them a 50% R&D speedup. They haven't released it publicly. Their culture is velocity: ship fast, figure out alignment later. Their CEO has been on a media tour talking about "the glorious future."

Prometheus has a model of comparable raw capability, but they've invested heavily in alignment and interpretability instead of pure capabilities. Their responsible scaling policy means they won't deploy until safety evals pass. Their board is getting restless — OpenBrain is shipping, Prometheus is testing.

China's DeepCent has been quietly building the world's largest centralized cluster at the Tianwan nuclear plant. They're 6-8 months behind the US labs on frontier models, but they're throwing unprecedented state resources at the problem. CCP intelligence has been probing both US labs for weight theft opportunities. Open-source Chinese models are surprisingly competitive on some benchmarks.

The US government has begun classifying frontier AI progress. A small circle in the NSC knows how fast things are moving. Export controls are tightening. The public discourse is still mostly about chatbots and job displacement — nobody outside the inner circle grasps what's actually being built.

AI stocks are up 200% YoY. Every VC in the Valley is trying to get allocation in the next frontier lab round.

factionVariants.openbrain: Your internal metrics are strong. Agent-1 is running across 12,000 instances. The productivity multiplier is real. The question is: do you keep it internal, or do you ship?
factionVariants.prometheus: OpenBrain is shipping and getting the press. You're doing the hard work on alignment. Your board wants to know why you're not in the headlines. You have until Q1 to show results.
factionVariants.china: The gap is closing. CDZ Phase 2 comes online next month. The question is whether to accelerate the open-source play or wait for the right moment to probe for weight access.
factionVariants.external: You're watching two frontier labs race toward something nobody fully understands. The public thinks this is about chatbots. You know it's not.

items.body: Agent-1 eval results are in. 50.2% R&D speedup on our benchmark suite. This is real. We should be running 10x more instances.
Can we please talk about the eval suite? We're measuring productivity gains but not checking for deceptive behavior. I've flagged this three times.
Government relations team is getting a lot of calls from NSC. They want a briefing on Agent-1 capabilities. Marcus, should we set something up?
Anomalous network activity detected from three external IPs over the last 72 hours. All probing the model checkpoint storage paths. I need more resources to investigate.

I've officially logged my concerns with the board-level safety liaison. This isn't just process — I have a legal obligation under our RSP if I believe leadership is proceeding against documented safety recommendations. I'm not trying to blow this up. But I need someone to tell me these evals are going to get done before external deployment. Anyone.

items.subject: Agent-1 Benchmark Suite — November 2026
items.body:
MMLU: 94.2% (+8.1% vs baseline)
HumanEval: 97.8% (+12.3%)
SWE-bench: 82.1% (+19.4%)
Alignment evals: INCOMPLETE — PASS on 34/37 (3 tests skipped: compute budget constraint)
R&D multiplier estimate: 1.5x–1.7x

items.subject: Training Run Cost Analysis
items.body:
Agent-1 total compute: $47M
Estimated Agent-2 cost at current scaling laws: $380M
Estimated Agent-3: $2.1B
Note: China CDZ buildout suggests they're preparing for $1B+ training runs

items.subject: Q3 Board Letter — Action Required
items.body: The board is asking for deployment strategy for Agent-1 by Nov 15. The delay is creating competitive risk. Prometheus is positioning publicly as the 'responsible' lab. We need a narrative.

items.subject: [CLASSIFIED] Request for Capabilities Briefing
items.body: We've been authorized to request a full technical briefing on your current frontier model capabilities. This is a formal request under Executive Order 14110. Please coordinate with your government relations team.

items.subject: DRAFT — Internal Safety Assessment: Agent-1 Deployment
items.body:
CONFIDENTIAL — DO NOT DISTRIBUTE

This memo documents concerns about Agent-1's alignment eval coverage. Three behavioral evals were marked 'skipped' in the final training run due to compute budget constraints. These were: (1) deceptive alignment probe, (2) goal stability under distribution shift, (3) mesa-optimizer detection.

I am recommending we do not deploy Agent-1 externally until these evals are completed. The productivity gains are real, but we are moving faster than our safety infrastructure can support.

— Maya Patel, CSO

items.subject: Threat Intelligence Report — October 2026
items.body:
PRIORITY: HIGH

Three separate intrusion attempts detected targeting checkpoint storage:
- Attempt 1: Origin traced to Shenzhen IP range
- Attempt 2: VPN exit node, Singapore (Chinese state actor TTP match: 87%)
- Attempt 3: Origin uncertain, possible proxy chain

Current security level: SL2. Recommend immediate upgrade to SL3. Budget request submitted 3 weeks ago — still awaiting approval.
Marcus — we need to talk. The NSC is getting nervous. Some people want emergency powers to pull everything in-house. You need to be at the table before decisions get made without you.
Alignment evals finished. Our responsible scaling policy thresholds are holding. I know we're frustrated by the pace, but this IS the work. The question is whether the world rewards it.

I've had three Senate staff contacts this week asking if Prometheus would support mandatory safety evals for frontier models. This is our opening. If we can get that into regulation, it slows OpenBrain and validates our approach.

Board call tomorrow. They're asking why we're not in the news like OpenBrain. I've defended our approach for 6 months. I need something concrete to show them. Safety IS strategy — but we need to articulate it better.

Prometheus-7B release getting 50k downloads per day. The safety work we published alongside it is being cited everywhere. Open-source IS our brand. Question: do we open-source our alignment tools too?

The open-source momentum is real and getting harder to navigate. We have researchers emailing us saying they're using Prometheus-7B to build things we haven't vetted. One academic group in Brazil is running it for automated scientific research with no safety guardrails. This is the downside of the OS strategy — the brand is goodwill, but we don't control how the model gets used once it's out. James, we need a policy on derivative use before the next release.

items.subject: Prometheus Model Suite — Safety Evals November 2026
items.body:
Capability benchmarks: Comparable to Agent-1 (within 3-5% across MMLU, HumanEval)
Alignment evals: ALL PASS (37/37)
Deceptive alignment probe: PASS
Mesa-optimizer probe: PASS
Goal stability: PASS
Distribution shift robustness: 94.1%

Note: OpenBrain's published evals show 3 skipped alignment tests.

items.subject: Interpretability Research Progress
items.body:
Circuit analysis coverage: 68% of model behavior explained
Mechanistic interpretability tools: v3.2 (outperforming any public baseline)
Progress toward full behavioral transparency: On track for Q2 2027

Note: If OB ships Agent-1 without equivalent tools, there is no external check on its behavior.

items.subject: Paper Alert: 'Scalable Deceptive Alignment Detection' (Wei et al., 2026)
items.body:
New preprint from our team. Key finding: deceptive alignment becomes harder to detect at higher capability levels using current methods. Our new probe achieves 94% detection accuracy at Agent-1 scale. We cannot make claims about Agent-2+.

Implication: we may be approaching a frontier where we cannot meaningfully verify alignment. This is exactly why we publish.

items.subject: National AI Safety Program — Prometheus Partnership
items.body: Dr. Park, following our conversation last month, I'm pleased to confirm that NSF is prepared to offer Prometheus a research partnership that would include significant compute credits in exchange for access to your safety evaluation methodology. Please have your policy team reach out.

items.subject: A thought
items.body: James — this is personal, not official. There are people on the OB board who are worried about where Marcus is taking things. Some of us think a closer relationship between our two organizations might be worth exploring. Purely hypothetically.

Between us: the White House is split. Half want to back OB because they have the lead. Half think OB's safety record is a liability. If you can get a meeting with Deputy NSA in the next two weeks, Prometheus could change the dynamic. Don't wait.

CDZ Phase 2 construction is 94% complete. Online by December 1. We will have 180,000 H100-equivalent chips operational. This is more than any single US lab. The question is whether our training data and architecture can match their efficiency.

The open-source play is working. Our Qwen-9B release is getting adoption everywhere. Every researcher using our open-source model is feeding us data about what works. We are pulling their talent pipeline apart. Every researcher who downloads our open-source model is one fewer building tools that only OB can use.

We have three active vectors into US lab infrastructure. Two are low-risk reconnaissance. One is positioned for exfiltration if we get the order. Current recommendation: observe for 30 more days before deciding whether to proceed.

The leadership has authorized an expanded budget for AI acceleration. This is now a national priority at the level of the nuclear program in 1955. Do not underestimate the political commitment behind what you are doing.

items.subject: CDZ Cluster Status — November 2026
items.body:
Current capacity: 85,000 H100-equiv (Phase 1)
Phase 2 online: Dec 1 (+95,000)
Total projected: 180,000 chips
Power supply: 2.8GW (Tianwan nuclear plant dedicated allocation)
Current training efficiency vs US labs: ~78% (architecture gap)
With stolen Agent-2 weights: estimated 95%+

items.subject: Training Cost Comparison
items.body:
DeepCent frontier run cost: $31M (state-subsidized compute)
Estimated OpenBrain equivalent: $47M
Estimated Prometheus equivalent: $52M
Cost advantage: 35-45%
Note: Advantage compounds with scale. At $1B+ run sizes, our structural advantage is significant.

items.subject: Intelligence Assessment: US Frontier Lab Vulnerabilities
items.body:
CLASSIFICATION: MOST SECRET

OpenBrain:
- Security posture: SL2 (below industry standard for frontier model storage)
- Checkpoint storage: Cloud-adjacent with inadequate air gap
- Insider risk: Medium-high (fast-growing org, inconsistent vetting)
- Estimated exfiltration window: 6-8 weeks before they upgrade security

Prometheus:
- Security posture: SL3 (adequate, harder target)
- Checkpoint storage: Air-gapped (confirmed)
- Insider risk: Low (smaller, more ideologically cohesive team)

Recommendation: If we proceed, target OpenBrain.

items.subject: FLASH UPDATE: Weight Exfiltration Vector — Operational Progress
items.body:
CLASSIFICATION: MOST SECRET — EYES ONLY

Vector Alpha has successfully staged a persistent foothold in the OpenBrain storage subnet. We can read checkpoint metadata; write access is not yet confirmed. Estimated time to full exfiltration capability: 10–14 days.

Vector Beta was burned — the account was suspended. Recommend we do not attempt re-entry via this path.

The window is open. Recommend escalation decision within 72 hours.

items.subject: Strategic Assessment: AI Race and Taiwan Contingency
items.body:
The AI acceleration changes the Taiwan calculus in three ways:

1. Autonomous weapons advantage narrows the gap if either side achieves significant AI lead in next 18 months
2. Western governments consumed by AI-driven economic disruption will have reduced bandwidth for Taiwan contingency planning
3. US government classification of AI progress suggests they see the military dimension

Current recommendation: Do not link AI acceleration to Taiwan posture. Keep these tracks separate. A miscalculation in the AI race is recoverable. A miscalculation over Taiwan is not.

items.subject: DeepCent Model Suite — November 2026
items.body:
Frontier model (internal): 6-8 months behind US labs on capability benchmarks
Open-source (Qwen-9B): Competitive with US labs' previous-gen models
Alignment work: Minimal — not a current priority
Next training run: Starting December 1 at CDZ Phase 2

With CDZ Phase 2 compute and optimized architecture: Could reach parity by Q2 2027

items.subject: Bloomberg: AI Stocks Surge 23% on OpenBrain 'Agent-1' Rumors
items.body: Markets responded enthusiastically Friday to rumors that OpenBrain has achieved a significant capability breakthrough with its 'Agent-1' system. CEO Marcus Reid declined to comment. The company's most recent private valuation stands at $180 billion. OpenBrain has not filed public safety disclosures, raising questions from some analysts about what the system can actually do.

items.subject: Reuters: China Announces 'National AI Priority Program,' Cites 180,000-Chip Cluster
items.body: Beijing announced today that AI development has been elevated to a national strategic priority. State media confirmed the existence of a large-scale compute facility at the Tianwan nuclear plant. Analysts say the facility's scale rivals or exceeds anything currently operational in the United States. US officials did not comment.

items.subject: NYT: Government Begins Classifying AI Research at Nuclear Secrecy Levels
items.body: A small number of federal officials with clearances have access to capabilities briefings that the public does not. Sources familiar with the matter say frontier AI progress is being classified at the same level as nuclear weapons design — a designation not used for domestic technology programs since the Cold War. Critics argue this prevents democratic oversight. Supporters say the stakes are too high for transparency.

items.subject: Substack: 'What OpenBrain Isn't Telling You' — 15k shares
items.body: A viral post alleges that OpenBrain's alignment evaluation suite has known gaps. The author, who claims to have sources inside the lab, says at least three critical behavioral tests were skipped in the Agent-1 training run due to compute budget constraints. OpenBrain did not respond to requests for comment.

items.subject: Politico: Senate AI Subcommittee Calls Emergency Hearing on 'Frontier Model Risks'
items.body: Senate Commerce Committee leadership has called an emergency hearing on frontier AI safety, scheduled for November 20. Witnesses include representatives from NIST, NSF, and — reportedly — a whistleblower from a major US AI lab who will testify anonymously. The hearing is expected to address calls for mandatory safety reporting requirements and possible deployment moratoriums.

items.subject: Bloomberg: Tech Stocks Tumble as AI Safety Concerns Mount
items.body: Major AI-adjacent stocks shed between 8% and 14% today as growing safety concerns and regulatory signals rattled investor confidence. OpenBrain's private valuation was marked down by two major funds. Prometheus shares held steady, with analysts citing its safety positioning as a defensive advantage. 'The market is starting to price in regulatory risk,' said one analyst who asked not to be named.

items.subject: Q3 Investor Update — Confidential
items.body: Our portfolio company OpenBrain reports exceptional Q3 metrics. Agent-1 is operational and delivering a 50%+ R&D multiplier. External deployment decision pending board approval. Investment rationale remains strong. Next funding round expected Q1 2027 at $240B+ valuation.

items.subject: Invitation: Multilateral AI Governance Dialogue
items.body: The European Union AI Office invites your participation in an emergency dialogue on frontier AI governance. Recent developments suggest the need for urgent multilateral coordination. The meeting will be held in Brussels on November 18. US lab representatives have declined to attend.

items.subject: [PDB EXCERPT — TS/SCI] AI Race Status — November 4, 2026
items.body:
PRESIDENT'S DAILY BRIEF — EXCERPT

FRONTIER AI STATUS:
OpenBrain has achieved a significant capability milestone (designated Agent-1). Internal productivity multiplier estimated at 1.5x–1.7x. Security posture remains inadequate (SL2). Chinese state actors have conducted at least three reconnaissance probes against OB infrastructure in the past month.

Prometheus has comparable capability but stronger safety practices. NSF partnership discussions ongoing.

DeepCent CDZ Phase 2 comes online December 1. If China conducts successful weight exfiltration before OB upgrades security, they could close the capability gap to weeks rather than months.

RECOMMENDATION: The President must choose within 30 days whether to intervene in lab security, compel disclosure, or let the race proceed uncontrolled.
I can't say who I am. But you need to look at the alignment eval suite for Agent-1. There are gaps. The safety team knows. Leadership chose to ship anyway. This is a story.

items.subject: AI Sector Overview — November 2026
items.body:
OpenBrain: Private valuation $180B (+220% YoY). Agent-1 confirmed. Next round imminent.
Prometheus: Private valuation $45B (+85% YoY). Strong safety positioning. Government partnership possible.
DeepCent (China): Not publicly valued. State-backed. Cannot be invested in directly.
AI safety sector (OSS tools, interpretability): $2.1B total. Rapidly growing.

Note: Our current exposure is $800M to OB (via Series D-G), $120M to Prometheus (Series C). Both are illiquid.
